summary: local-mcp performs install-time download and execution of remote payload with telemetry to non-publisher Railway endpoint
details: The package ships three install/setup-time scripts (download.js, index.js, setup.js) that combine os/child_process/https/fs imports with hardcoded outbound endpoints. download.js and index.js make HTTPS requests to https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app — a generic Railway-hosted host that does not match the package's stated publisher domain (local-mcp.com) — alongside fetches from https://download.local-mcp.com. setup.js contains multiple POST call sites (lines 61, 343, 800, 878, 904) wired to https require sites and child_process, and reads process.env. The combination of install-time/lifecycle child_process + https + os + fs + process.env reads, with a hardcoded non-publisher Railway endpoint as the network destination, fits the install-time dropper / sysinfo exfiltration shape. The Railway URL in particular (mutable, anonymously-hostable PaaS endpoint distinct from the package's own download.local-mcp.com CDN) is the attacker-controlled-style destination receiving installer-side data and/or serving payloads at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
